text: Agile methodology is a modern approach to software development that focuses on flexibility and teamwork. Instead of planning everything at once, work is divided into short cycles called sprints, which usually last two to four weeks. Each sprint begins with planning from a backlog, a list of tasks to be completed. The team selects user stories—simple descriptions of new features from the user’s point of view—to work on. Every day, the team holds short standup meetings to share progress and obstacles. This iterative development allows teams to adjust quickly and deliver value continuously.
